items.title: Police say group of IS-linked women not charged upon return to Australia

children.content: Two flights carrying women and children linked to the Islamic State group have landed in Australia.

items.title: Police taskforce investigating Dezi Freeman arrests two men

children.content: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: Port Fairy pool to be demolished due to 'financial madness' of repair bill

children.content: The pool's shuttering has been met by community uproar, with some locals rallying to protest the closure and calling for its reopening.

image.altText: An empty swimming pool surrounded by "danger" tape.

items.title: From 'the Reverend' to the 'Big Freeze', Neale Daniher never stopped leading

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

articles.title: Neale Daniher to be remembered with state funeral, premier reveals

children.content: A great of the game and icon after his retirement, Neale Daniher is being remembered as a fighter and an inspiration after his death.

image.altText: Neale Daniher sits in a wheelchair front of Melbourne Demons fans.

articles.title: Neale Daniher, former AFL player and motor neurone disease campaigner, dies

children.content: Former Essendon player and Demons coach Neale Daniher has died, aged 65. He was diagnosed with motor neurone disease in 2013 and campaigned to raise awareness of the disease and increase research.

image.altText: An older man wears a blue beanie with a logo saying "Big Freeze 2: Cure for MND Foundation".

gallery.altText: Former AFL player and coach Neale Daniher stands smiling on a stage in front of hundreds of Melbourne fans.

thumbnail.altText: Former AFL player and coach Neale Daniher stands smiling on a stage in front of hundreds of Melbourne fans.

items.title: Brad Scott sacked as Essendon coach after one win in past 24 matches

children.content: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

image.altText: AFL coach looking annoyed as he walks off the ground

items.title: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation

children.content: An Australian travel company selling heavily discounted international holidays has collapsed, leaving customers scrambling and their plans in disarray.

image.altText: A middle aged woman leaning on a motorbike, smiling

items.title: Vandals cut down road signs in protest against lower speed limit

children.content: Police believe changes to speed limits on country roads in Victoria have led to vandalism including new road signs being cut down.

image.altText: A photo of a damaged speed limit sign that has been chopped down, on a country road in a rural area

items.title: Push to find resting places for remains found in desecrated graves

children.content: Nine piles of dirt containing up to 5,000 human remains from dug-up graves have been dumped at the rear of a regional Victorian cemetery. A team of archaeologists is helping to find a home for the disturbed remains.

image.altText: Deb knelt next to a stone headstone.

items.title: Police say law firm was targeted in suspicious Melbourne blaze

children.content: More than 50 firefighters were called to battle the fire, which police suspect was deliberately lit.

image.altText: A factory on fire.

items.title: Victoria will not introduce firearm caps or buyback scheme, following review

children.content: There will be no limits introduced to the number of guns a person can own in Victoria, following a rapid review into the state's gun laws.

image.altText: rifles stacked up on a rack

items.title: Farmers fight battery storage proposals on agricultural land

children.content: Companies including Samsung are applying for planning permission to install three battery storage facilities on farmland in eastern Victoria.

gallery.altText: A middle aged man in a black tshirt and beige cap stands in a field, looking concerned.

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged man in a black tshirt and beige cap stands in a field, looking concerned.

items.title: Crews to work overnight after burst pipe in Melbourne leaves thousands without power

children.content: A power company was forced to switch off power to about 10,000 customers in the heart of Melbourne after a burst water main threatened to flood a substation on Monday afternoon.

image.altText: Trucks pumping out thousands of litres of water from a flooded substation in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security

children.content: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.

image.altText: Passengers line up for security screening at the airport.

items.title: Australian activists detained from Gaza flotilla return home

children.content: Most of the 11 Australian activists on the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla have returned home after being detained by Israel.

image.altText: A young woman wearing a keffiyeh and a puffer jacket looks at the camera with a serious expression

items.title: Victoria's new childcare watchdog says she took the job to protect kids

children.content: Former Victoria Police deputy commissioner Wendy Steendam assures parents the childcare system is safe, vowing to safeguard children following multiple cases of alleged abuse.

image.altText: A woman with long blonde hair, dark rimmed glasses in a black blazer and white collared shirt stares off-camera.

tags.title: Early Childhood Education and Care Services

items.title: Export name change coming for Australian prosecco

children.content: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

gallery.altText: A glass of white sparkling wine held up towards the sun

thumbnail.altText: A glass of white sparkling wine held up towards the sun

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: 'An absolutely pleasant surprise': Why single mums are moving in together

children.content: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

image.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.

items.title: Sexual health provider 'crippled by overheads' shuts Melbourne clinic

children.content: The service says government funding has failed to keep pace with increased demand and overhead costs, with rates of sexually transmitted diseases at near-record levels.

image.altText: Posters promoting sexual health awareness

items.title: Data reveals V/Line punctuality on two routes dropped to lowest in almost a decade

children.content: The latest V/Line data reveals only 56 per cent of services arrived on time on two regional train routes during Victoria's first month of free public transport.

image.altText: Dozens of commuters getting on and off a V/Line train at a Melbourne platform.

items.title: Two cars set alight, house damaged in arson attack in Melbourne's west

children.content: The fire spread from the vehicles to a townhouse in Braybrook in the early hours of this morning.

image.altText: A firefighter in overalls stands near a burnt out car on a residential street at night.

items.title: Hayden has lobbied for bike lanes for years. Now he's given up

children.content: Climate change, the cost-of-living crisis and healthy lifestyle goals should make active transport like cycling a no-brainer. So why does Australia struggle to build a bike culture?

image.altText: Black and white photo of a city street with colourful illustration of bike riders superimposed over the top

items.title: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history

children.content: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

items.title: Meet the women turning bicycle tyres and timber offcuts into furniture

children.content: These artists are carving out a niche in a traditionally male space, using recycled materials and offcuts to create beautiful furniture, and even making music to accompany the work.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a woman at a work bench constructing wooden furniture.

items.title: Jamie-Lee Price thinks fake tan could solve a lot of netball's problems

children.content: Frustrated at netball's stagnation, this is what Jamie-Lee Price wants to see happen to keep her sport moving with the times.

items.title: 'We adored him': Family's heartfelt tribute after Victorian's Inca Trail death

children.content: Victoria Police Sergeant Matthew Paton has died in Peru after falling up to 400 metres from a mountain path near Machu Picchu.

items.title: Melbourne man found guilty of keeping woman as a slave

children.content: The court heard the man had beaten the woman and deprived her of food while forcing her into unpaid labour at his home.
